target.title: feat(feishu): add list action and per-agent routing for feishu_chat
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `list` action to the `feishu_chat` tool, enabling agents to list their recent chats (groups and DMs) with pagination and sort support
- Switch from hardcoded first-account client to per-agent account routing via `createFeishuToolClient`, so each agent uses its own bound Feishu account in multi-agent setups
- Make `chat_id` optional (required for `members`/`info`, not needed for `list`)
- Add `accountId` param to allow explicit account override

## Changed files
- `extensions/feishu/src/chat-schema.ts` — extended schema with `list` action, `sort_type`, `user_id_type`, optional `chat_id` and `accountId`
- `extensions/feishu/src/chat.ts` — added `getChatList` handler, switched to `createFeishuToolClient` with per-agent routing
- `extensions/feishu/src/chat.test.ts` — rewrote tests using `createToolFactoryHarness`, added coverage for list action, pagination, error handling, missing `chat_id` validation, and per-agent/explicit account routing

## Test plan
- [x] All 9 unit tests passing (`pnpm test -- extensions/feishu/src/chat.test.ts`)
- [ ] Manual verification in a multi-agent Feishu environment

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

target.title: feat(exec): add bubblewrap namespace sandbox for safeBins commands
target.body:
## What

Adds kernel-level filesystem isolation for safeBins-approved commands using [bubblewrap](https://github.com/containers/bubblewrap) (bwrap) unprivileged user namespaces on Linux.

## Why

The current safeBins fence validates command names but can't prevent absolute-path bypasses (e.g. `/usr/bin/rm`). With bwrap, the binary simply doesn't exist inside the sandbox — filesystem isolation is enforced by the kernel, not by argument parsing.

## How

When `tools.exec.nsSandbox.mode` is set to `"bwrap"`, commands that pass safeBins validation are wrapped in a bwrap namespace where only:

- **Approved binaries** — resolved from `trustedSafeBinDirs`, mounted read-only
- **Shell binaries** — `sh`, `bash`, `env` always available for `sh -c`
- **System libraries** — `/lib`, `/usr/lib` etc. for dynamic linking (read-only)
- **System config** — SSL certs, DNS, locale (read-only)
- **Working directory** — mounted read-write
- **Extra mounts** — configurable via `nsSandbox.extraBinds`

Everything else is invisible. Namespace isolation uses `--unshare-all --share-net --die-with-parent`.

### Interaction with other security layers

| State | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Trust window active | No sandbox (run directly) |
| No trust + safeBins match + bwrap enabled | Wrapped in bwrap namespace |
| No trust + safeBins match + bwrap disabled | Run directly (existing behavior) |
| No trust + not safeBins | Ask for approval (existing behavior) |
| Docker sandbox (`host=sandbox`) | Docker takes precedence, no bwrap |
| PTY requested | Skip bwrap (terminal allocation unreliable in namespaces) |

Composes cleanly with trust windows (PR #39126).

### Config

```jsonc
{
  "tools": {
    "exec": {
      "nsSandbox": {
        "mode": "bwrap",  // "bwrap" | "none" (default)
        "extraBinds": [
          { "src": "/opt/data", "writable": false },
          { "src": "/opt/output", "writable": true }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Requires bubblewrap installed (`bwrap`). Ships with Fedora/Ubuntu via Flatpak deps. Falls back to no-sandbox gracefully if not available.

## Files changed (8)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/infra/exec-bwrap-sandbox.ts` | **New** — Core module: bwrap arg builder, detection, config normalization |
| `src/infra/exec-bwrap-sandbox.test.ts` | **New** — 18 unit tests |
| `src/config/types.tools.ts` | Add `nsSandbox` to `ExecToolConfig` |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts` | Add `nsSandbox` to `ExecToolDefaults` |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` | Add bwrap spawn path in `runExecProcess` |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts` | Build bwrap params when safeBins match |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts` | Thread bwrap params from gateway → runtime |
| `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` | Thread `nsSandbox` config through defaults |

## Testing

- 18 new unit tests for bwrap module (arg building, mount dedup, config normalization, binary resolution)
- 47 existing safeBins tests still passing
- `tsc --noEmit` — zero errors from our changes
- Manual: bwrap v0.11.0 on Fedora 43

## AI Disclosure

AI-assisted (Tommy/Claude Opus 4.6). All code reviewed and understood by contributor.

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Control UI could restore a historical routed session key like `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` from persisted local settings after reload.
- Why it matters: users would reopen Control UI and land in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI look misrouted even though the backend heartbeat target remained main.
- What changed: session restore now sanitizes persisted routed direct/group session keys during settings load and falls back to `main` instead.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway routing behavior, heartbeat behavior, or live session switching logic was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61661
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI restoring historical routed direct/group sessions from persisted local settings instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```
- Observed result after fix: persisted `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` session selections are normalized back to `main` during settings restore.
- What was not tested: explicit session selection through URL query params or live backend routing changes, because this PR only changes local restore behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Control UI storage restore trusted persisted `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` values without filtering out historical routed direct/group sessions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: storage tests covered scoped session persistence but did not cover stale routed-session restore behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): main-session normalization already existed later in the gateway boot path, but the bad routed session had already won at the storage restore layer.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: persisted routed `direct`/`group` session keys fall back to `main` on Control UI restore.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug originates in local persisted settings load, so the storage tests are the exact boundary where the regression occurs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds two restore-specific cases for routed `direct` and `group` sessions.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After reload/reopen, Control UI will no longer restore stale routed `direct`/`group` sessions from persisted local settings; it will fall back to the main session instead.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI local storage restore

### Steps

1. Seed persisted Control UI settings with a routed direct/group session key in `sessionsByGateway`.
2. Call `loadSettings()`.
3. Verify restored `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` fall back to `main`.

### Expected

- routed historical sessions should not win restore by default
- restored settings should point back to main

### Actual

- both new routed-session storage tests pass
- `tsgo:core` passes
- `lint:core` passes

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the restore sanitization happens in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`, which is the correct layer for this bug.
- Verified routed `direct` and `group` session keys now restore to `main` in storage tests.
- Verified core type/lint checks pass on the changed files.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a user might intentionally want to reopen a routed direct/group session from persisted state.
  - Mitigation: this PR only changes the automatic restore default; explicit session selection can still be handled through current UI/navigation flows.
- Risk: over-matching could reset legitimate session keys.
  - Mitigation: the sanitization is narrowly limited to `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` patterns only.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  ui  ../../ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts (22 tests) 38ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  22 passed (22)
    Start at  00:14:43
    Duration  611ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Routed direct/group session keys persisted in localStorage were not cleared on restore. The regex `/^agent:[^:]+:[^:]+:(direct|group):.+$/` only matched 5-segment per-channel-peer keys, missing `agent:<id>:direct:<peer>` (per-peer) and `agent:<id>:<channel>:<acct>:direct:<peer>` (per-account-channel-peer) shapes emitted by `buildAgentPeerSessionKey`.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, jsdom, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 22/22 tests pass including two new tests covering per-peer (`agent:main:direct:ou_123`) and per-account-channel-peer (`agent:main:feishu:acct_abc:direct:ou_123`) shapes, both correctly falling back to `main`.
**Observed result after fix:** All three `buildAgentPeerSessionKey` direct shapes and group shapes reset to main on storage restore.
**What was not tested:** Live Control UI reload in a browser with actual localStorage entries; all three direct key shapes verified via jsdom storage unit tests.

target.title: fix #84079: normalize SendMessage/content/text aliases to message before send validation
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #84079

### Issue
[`message` tool rejects model-generated `SendMessage` instead of normalizing it to `message`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84079)

### Changes
- Normalize common text-body aliases (SendMessage, content, text) to the canonical `message` field before send validation
- Add diagnostic warning log when alias normalization is applied
- Add comprehensive tests for alias normalization

### Changed Files
```
 CHANGELOG.md                                       |  1 +
 src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts        | 11 +++
 src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts | 85 +++++++++++++++++++++-
 3 files changed, 96 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

## Real behavior proof

- [x] **Diff size**: S (96 lines)
- [x] Tests cover: SendMessage alias, content alias, text alias, no-op when message already set, priority order

<!-- codex-real-behavior-proof:start -->

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior addressed

PR #84102 fixes message send body alias handling so `SendMessage`, `content`, and `text` are normalized to `message` before the actual outbound send path validates and dispatches a `send` action. This proof verifies the aliases reach the real `runMessageAction -> executeSendAction -> sendMessage -> sendDurableMessageBatch -> outbound adapter sendText` path, not just a unit-level parameter check.

### Real environment tested

- Date/time: 2026-05-20 00:21-00:27 Asia/Shanghai
- Host: Linux LIN-404B39D3E3F.zte.intra 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 x86_64
- Node: v22.22.0
- pnpm: 11.1.0
- Worktree: `/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079`
- Current PR head SHA: `2b86487467c57e6cd9ffd1c96dea5f762b5b180a`
- Isolated OpenClaw home: `/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home`
- Isolated OpenClaw state/config:
  - `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home/state`
  - `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home/state/openclaw.json`
- OpenClaw port: `19102`
- Gateway runtime log path reported by OpenClaw: `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-20.log`
- Local proof artifacts/log excerpts are under `.automation/` in this worktree and are intentionally not committed.

### OpenClaw startup command

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home/state/openclaw.json \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19102 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
pnpm openclaw gateway --port 19102 --verbose --allow-unconfigured
```

First attempt with `pnpm gateway:dev` correctly failed before listen because the isolated empty config was missing `gateway.mode`; the second command above follows the gateway's own `--allow-unconfigured` instruction and keeps the run isolated from the already-running personal Co-Claw/OpenClaw instance.

### OpenClaw startup evidence

Gateway output from the #84102 worktree:

```text
2026-05-20T00:21:27.331+08:00 [gateway] loading configuration...
2026-05-20T00:21:27.574+08:00 [gateway] resolving authentication...
2026-05-20T00:21:27.611+08:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-20T00:21:28.891+08:00 [gateway] auth token was missing. Generated a runtime token for this startup without changing config.
2026-05-20T00:21:34.027+08:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
2026-05-20T00:21:38.702+08:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-20T00:21:38.706+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 11.1s)
2026-05-19T21:38:10.761+08:00 [gateway] log file: /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-20.log
2026-05-20T00:21:38.718+08:00 [gateway/channels] skipping channel start (OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 or OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1)
2026-05-20T00:21:39.634+08:00 [browser/server] Browser control listening on http://127.0.0.1:19104/ (auth=token)
2026-05-20T00:21:39.652+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-20T00:21:39.665+08:00 [heartbeat] started
```

Listening proof:

```text
$ ss -ltnp | grep 19102
LISTEN 0      128             127.0.0.1:19102      0.0.0.0:*    users:(("openclaw",pid=1469069,fd=22))
LISTEN 0      128                 [::1]:19102         [::]:*    users:(("openclaw",pid=1469069,fd=23))
```

### Real send alias proof

Command:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84079/.automation/openclaw-home/state/openclaw.json \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19102 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
node --import tsx .automation/proof-message-alias.ts
```

The proof script registers a temporary `proofsend` channel plugin with a real direct outbound `sendText` adapter, then calls `runMessageAction({ action: "send", dryRun: false })` for each alias. The adapter records the text it receives and returns a message id.

Output:

```text
[message-tool] normalized alias "SendMessage" to "message" for send action
{"alias":"SendMessage","result":{"kind":"send","channel":"proofsend","to":"C84102","handledBy":"core","dryRun":false,"sendResult":{"channel":"proofsend","to":"C84102","via":"direct","mediaUrl":null,"result":{"channel":"proofsend","messageId":"proof-1"}}},"delivered":{"to":"C84102","text":"alias via SendMessage"},"warning":"[message-tool] normalized alias \"SendMessage\" to \"message\" for send action"}
[message-tool] normalized alias "content" to "message" for send action
{"alias":"content","result":{"kind":"send","channel":"proofsend","to":"C84102","handledBy":"core","dryRun":false,"sendResult":{"channel":"proofsend","to":"C84102","via":"direct","mediaUrl":null,"result":{"channel":"proofsend","messageId":"proof-2"}}},"delivered":{"to":"C84102","text":"alias via content"},"warning":"[message-tool] normalized alias \"content\" to \"message\" for send action"}
[message-tool] normalized alias "text" to "message" for send action
{"alias":"text","result":{"kind":"send","channel":"proofsend","to":"C84102","handledBy":"core","dryRun":false,"sendResult":{"channel":"proofsend","to":"C84102","via":"direct","mediaUrl":null,"result":{"channel":"proofsend","messageId":"proof-3"}}},"delivered":{"to":"C84102","text":"alias via text"},"warning":"[message-tool] normalized alias \"text\" to \"message\" for send action"}
{"missingMessageRejected":true,"error":"message required"}
```

### Verification commands

Targeted test:

```text
$ pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  16 passed (16)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.72s
```

Changed checks:

```text
$ pnpm check:changed
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests, docs, tooling
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
Exit code: 0
```

Diff scope:

```text
CHANGELOG.md                                       |  1 +
src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts  | 85 +++++++++++++++++++++-
src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts        | 11 +++
3 files changed, 96 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

### Observed result after fix

- The OpenClaw gateway from this PR worktree started successfully on isolated port `19102` and reached `[gateway] ready`.
- Each alias (`SendMessage`, `content`, `text`) emitted the normalization diagnostic, then reached the actual outbound direct send path with `dryRun:false`.
- The direct outbound adapter received the expected message text and returned message ids `proof-1`, `proof-2`, and `proof-3`.
- A send with no `message` and no alias still rejects with `message required`, so the fix does not loosen empty-message validation.
- Targeted validation and changed checks both passed.

### What was not tested

- No live external provider account was used. The proof intentionally uses an isolated test channel adapter to avoid sending real messages to Slack/Feishu/other services.
- Channels were skipped during startup with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1` to keep this PR proof isolated from local personal channel credentials and currently running Co-Claw/OpenClaw instances.
- Browser UI interaction was not required for this backend send-path fix.

target.title: fix(cron): capture originating session/agent on the cron wake tool call
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** The cron `wake` MCP tool forwards only `{mode, text}` to the gateway, so every wake enqueues against the heartbeat/main default. Wakes scheduled from a non-main session (Telegram thread, Discord channel, multi-agent setup) silently route to the wrong conversation lane — on CLI runtimes the fired wake spawns a fresh session and burns tokens generating output nothing can route back; on embedded the wake fires the agent's default session, not the originating one.
- **What changed.** Wake tool resolves `opts.agentSessionKey` via the existing `resolveInternalSessionKey` / `resolveSessionAgentId` pattern (mirrors the `add` action at L569-583) and threads `sessionKey` + `agentId` through `WakeParamsSchema` → gateway handler → `context.cron.wake` → `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeat`. Explicit params on the tool call take precedence over inferred values.
- **CLI runtime coverage (claude-cli + claude-cli-interactive).** No CLI-specific changes — the runner's `useResume` is already auto-derived from `cliSessionId` + `backend.resumeArgs` presence (`src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:276-277`). Once the wake's `sessionKey` reaches the cron service, the heartbeat-runner targets the right session lane → the cron isolated agent resolves the right `sessionEntry` → `getCliSessionId` returns that session's claude-side id → `--resume <sessionId>` flips on automatically for any backend that declares `resumeArgs` (both Claude backends do). Same path works for Codex, Gemini-CLI, etc.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** `WakeParamsSchema` still permits `additionalProperties: true`. Pre-existing call sites that omit origin still route to the dep's default (backwards compatible). Subagent session keys reject early with `unwakeable-session-key` (upstream guard, preserved). Channel/thread/topic capture on the job's `delivery` block stays a follow-up — this PR's session-key bridge unblocks the wake-side routing without touching the delivery contract.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** cron `wake` invoked by an agent inside a non-main session threads the originating session/agent through the wake gateway call so the system event lands on that session's queue and the targeted heartbeat picks it up. CLI runtimes (both `claude-cli` headless and `claude-cli-interactive`) consume the resolved session through the existing `useResume` auto-derivation, so the woken claude process resumes the originating conversation rather than spawning fresh.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, AJV-compiled TypeBox validators, vitest 4.1.6. Live harness in `scripts/proof-cron-wake-origin.mts` drives the patched gateway wake handler (`validateWakeParams` + `isSubagentSessionKey` guard) through the patched `cron.wake()` / `timer.ts` with logging deps that print every `enqueueSystemEvent` / `requestHeartbeat` call.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/wake-origin.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts && pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-cron-wake-origin.mts`.
- **Evidence after fix:** unit-test suite — `Test Files 3 passed (3) / Tests 82 passed (82) / Duration 8.05s`. Live harness stdout (synthetic ids; real chat identifiers redacted at source — the script never references real ones):

  ```
  === non-main session + non-default agent (the bug-fix case) ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"follow up on report","sessionKey":"agent:coding:telegram:<chat-id-redacted>:topic:<topic-id-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="follow up on report"  opts={"sessionKey":"agent:coding:telegram:<chat-id-redacted>:topic:<topic-id-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake","sessionKey":"agent:coding:telegram:<chat-id-redacted>:topic:<topic-id-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}

  === no origin (backwards-compatible default routing) ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"ping"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="ping"  opts=undefined
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}

  === next-heartbeat + sessionKey collapses to targeted-immediate ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"next-heartbeat","text":"check the queue","sessionKey":"agent:coding:discord:<thread-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="check the queue"  opts={"sessionKey":"agent:coding:discord:<thread-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake","sessionKey":"agent:coding:discord:<thread-redacted>","agentId":"coding"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}

  === subagent sessionKey rejected by gateway handler guard ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"wake my subagent","sessionKey":"subagent:scratch:<id-redacted>"}
  < wake result:  ok=false  payload=undefined
  < wake error:   {"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"wake sessionKey cannot target a subagent session"}

  === whitespace-only origin falls through (defence-in-depth) ===
  > wake params: {"mode":"now","text":"x","sessionKey":"   ","agentId":"\t"}
  [gateway/cron] enqueueSystemEvent  text="x"  opts=undefined
  [gateway/heartbeat] requestHeartbeat  {"source":"manual","intent":"immediate","reason":"wake"}
  < wake result:  ok=true  payload={"ok":true}
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** non-main wake calls hit `enqueueSystemEvent(text, {sessionKey, agentId})` and `requestHeartbeat({source: "manual", intent: "immediate", reason: "wake", sessionKey, agentId})` on the originating lane, not the heartbeat/main default. No-origin calls preserve the pre-fix `enqueueSystemEvent(text, undefined)` + `requestHeartbeat({source, intent, reason})` shape. `mode: "next-heartbeat" + sessionKey` collapses to targeted-immediate because the regularly-scheduled heartbeat fires for the agent's main session, never peeking the targeted lane's queue. Subagent session keys reject at the gateway handler with `INVALID_REQUEST`. Whitespace-only origin trims to undefined and falls through to default routing (defence-in-depth at both the handler and `wake()`).
- **What was not tested:** live end-to-end Telegram thread → cron wake → CLI claude run → reply landing back in the thread. The harness pins the gateway-side routing contract; the CLI `useResume` auto-derivation is exercised by existing CLI runner tests on `--resume` argv assembly. A full live-Telegram capture is a Mantis follow-up (it needs `delivery.{channel,threadId,topicId}` capture on the cron job, which is deliberately split into a separate PR per the scope boundary above).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Closes the upstream half of #46886 and #64556.
- Related (downstream slices): #70268, #57199, #82767, #79869, #63096. This PR fixes the upstream origin-capture they all silently assume.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** existing in-process callers of `wake({mode, text})` keep working only if origin remains optional.
  - **Mitigation:** `sessionKey` / `agentId` optional; omitted (or whitespace-only) falls through to the dep's default. Regression test pins both branches.
- **Risk:** new `NonEmptyString` slots on the schema reject empty-string origin from existing callers.
  - **Mitigation:** existing callers don't send these fields. `additionalProperties: true` unchanged so forward-compat metadata still passes.
- **Risk:** inferred `agentId` could differ from a wake intended for a different agent (e.g. operator scheduling a wake for `coding` from `main`).
  - **Mitigation:** explicit `sessionKey` / `agentId` params on the tool call override the inferred values. The implicit capture is the default for the "wake my own session" case.
- **Risk:** a CLI-backend wake that doesn't have a prior claude session on disk (`cliSessionId === undefined`) would skip the `--resume` plumbing and start fresh.
  - **Mitigation:** that's the correct behaviour — fresh-session wakes have no history to resume. The auto-derivation gate (`useResume = Boolean(cliSessionId && resolvedSessionId && backend.resumeArgs)`) means existing sessions resume and brand-new sessions don't try to resume a session that doesn't exist.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — session keys and agent ids are not credentials.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `wake` action; only the routing target changes.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — wake events still confined to in-process session queues; routing now goes to the correct queue.

target.title: feat(cli-output): tool-use injection + rolling-timer indicator for claude-stream-json backends
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** When Claude calls a tool mid-turn on the `claude-cli` backend, the assistant text stream just pauses — often for tens of seconds on `Read` / `Bash` / `Grep` / `WebFetch`. The end user sees a frozen typing indicator and no signal about which tool is running.
- **Why it matters.** Looks like the gateway hung; users abandon turns that are still in flight. Tool calls live in `stream_event` records, never in `assistant_text` deltas, so the existing assistant-stream consumer never surfaces them.
- **What changed.** Two complementary surfaces on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` (gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`):
  1. **In-stream textual marker** via `onAssistantDelta` — `\n\n[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail\n` followed by a rolling `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator that refreshes every 8s, stripped on the next `text_delta` or on turn end.
  2. **Structured `stream: "tool"` agent event** (`{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}`) via a new optional `onToolEvent` callback — consumed by `cli-runner/execute.ts` and forwarded to the agent event bus for downstream tool-tile UIs.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect({backend, providerId})` — no impact on non-Claude CLI backends. Existing `onAssistantDelta` callers are unaffected. `onToolEvent` is opt-in. No tool-output / tool-result event in this PR (the result is reflected in claude's next `text_delta` and the timer-strip handles UX). Tool args sanitized via `sanitizeToolArgs` before either path.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** tool calls during a Claude turn now produce both an in-stream textual marker (with rolling 8s timer indicator) and a structured `stream: "tool"` agent event, when `backend.jsonlDialect === "claude-stream-json"` or `providerId === "claude-cli"`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142, Opus 4.7, live OpenClaw gateway driving the `claude-cli` backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`; live Telegram turn that invokes one or more tools (Read / Bash / Grep / WebFetch); observe the textual marker, the 8s tick refresh, and the marker stripping plus `\n\n` separator when assistant text resumes; verify the `stream: "tool"` agent event in `emitAgentEvent` logs.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Telegram screenshot of a live turn (rolling-timer rendered to the end-user surface): ![telegram rolling-timer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-82285/.proof/telegram-rolling-timer.jpg). Every `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Bash: …` line in the screenshot is one `emitTool` firing in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` against `claude-cli`.
  - Typecheck clean: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.
  - The markers are an in-flight overlay delivered via `onAssistantDelta`, not persisted to `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` (which stores claude's structured `result`) or `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` at default verbosity — Telegram is the canonical capture surface.
- **Observed result after fix:** tool calls render as `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail` blocks in the assistant text, with the live timer ticking every 8s. When claude resumes streaming text, the timer is stripped and the text continues from a clean `\n\n` boundary. Agent-event consumers receive a structured `{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}` for each tool call.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a dashboard / external listener for the `stream: "tool"` event surface (event is emitted, visible in `emitAgentEvent` logs, but no downstream UI verified beyond that); Linux/macOS execution (developed on Windows only); bundled MCP tool paths (`server_tool_use` / `mcp_tool_use` content-block types are matched in the tracker but I have not driven a live MCP-tool turn through it); focused unit test for `createClaudeToolUseTracker` is not added yet (see Regression Test Plan).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Adds opt-in `onToolEvent` callback on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` + in-stream textual marker over the existing `onAssistantDelta`.
- Overlap to coordinate: #80046 (CLI tool-event bridge). Maintainers' judgment on whether inline rolling markers ride alongside or after the structured bridge.

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The condition this addresses (no tool-call UX during turns) is a property of the Claude CLI JSONL surface (tool calls live in `stream_event`, never in `assistant_text` deltas), not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` — add a `describe("tool-use tracker", …)` block.
- **Scenario locked in:** simulated `stream_event` sequence — `message_start` → `content_block_start (tool_use, name: "Read")` → 3× `content_block_delta (input_json_delta partial)` → `content_block_stop` — asserts the resulting `ClaudeToolEvent` carries the reassembled `args.file_path`, the right `itemId`, and that the parser's `assistantText` now includes the `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Read: <path>` line.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode (regression of partial-JSON accumulation, wrong `content_block_stop` dispatch, verbose-gating drift) is fully exercisable by calling the parser's `push()` with a canned JSONL transcript — no live claude needed. Runs in ms.
- **Honest gap:** I have **not** added this test yet. Behaviour is exercised live in my local environment but a frozen-transcript unit test belongs with this PR before flipping out of draft. Flagging as a must-add before un-drafting.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** partial-JSON accumulation in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` could drop or corrupt tool args if `content_block_stop` fires before all `input_json_delta` chunks arrive.
  - **Mitigation:** tracker holds the entry until `content_block_stop`, then parses `entry.input ?? entry.partialJson` via `parseClaudeToolArgs`. The pending unit test (`cli-output.test.ts`) locks in the contract for partial-JSON reassembly.
- **Risk:** rolling-timer `setInterval` leaks if a `content_block_stop` arrives but no subsequent `text_delta` fires (e.g. turn aborts mid-tool).
  - **Mitigation:** `clearToolKeepalive()` is also called on `result`, on `finish()`, and on the next `text_delta`. The parser closure owns the interval handle and explicitly clears it on every terminal path.
- **Risk:** tool args contain sensitive content (file paths, credentials in `WebFetch` URLs, etc.) and surface in both Telegram and agent events.
  - **Mitigation:** `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates and redacts known-sensitive keys before either emission path. Verbose gating (`shouldInjectToolInlineMarkers`) lets sessions opt out of inline markers without losing the structured event.
- **Risk:** the inline marker rides on `onAssistantDelta` — any consumer that strict-parses assistant text as Markdown could choke on the `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator.
  - **Mitigation:** marker uses a deliberately conservative `\n\n[…] 🛠️ …\n` shape that's Markdown-safe (Telegram renders it verbatim). Consumers wanting strict text can ignore by subscribing only to the structured `stream: "tool"` event.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — same allowed-tools set; `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates args before emission.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same tools claude already executes; this PR only renders that they're in flight.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — tracker reads `stream_event` records the parser already processes.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`; non-Claude backends unaffected.
- `onToolEvent` is an additive optional callback — existing parser callers don't need to wire it up.
- No new config, no new env vars. Disable by not subscribing to the new event surface.

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): validate CLI search limit to prevent NaN propagation
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The `ltm search` CLI command uses `parseInt(opts.limit)` without a radix parameter and without NaN validation. Passing a non-numeric value (e.g. `--limit abc`) causes `NaN` to propagate directly into the LanceDB `.limit()` call, leading to unpredictable query behavior.
- **Why it matters:** Users who mistype or pass invalid `--limit` values get a confusing LanceDB error instead of a graceful fallback.
- **What changed:** Added radix parameter (`10`) and a `Number.isFinite` + positive-integer guard that falls back to the default of `5` (matching the commander default on line 507).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No other CLI commands or search logic modified. Only the limit parsing for `ltm search`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #63326 (same `parseInt` → `Number.parseInt` pattern, but for `src/` files — this PR covers `extensions/memory-lancedb`)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `parseInt(opts.limit)` was used without a radix parameter and without validating the parsed result. JavaScript's `parseInt("abc")` returns `NaN`, which then flows unchecked into `db.search(vector, NaN, 0.3)`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No input validation on the CLI option before passing to the database layer.
- **Contributing context (if known):** The established pattern in `src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.ts:72-73` already uses `Number.parseInt` with radix and `Number.isFinite` guard — this extension predates that pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test

target.title: CLI: add --all flag to models status --probe for per-model health check
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `--all` flag to `openclaw models status --probe` that probes every configured model individually, not just one per provider
- Catches deprecated/renamed model IDs that pass auth-only validation because the API key is valid

## Problem
`models status --probe` validates provider auth by picking one representative model per provider via `selectProbeModel()`. This means:
- Deprecated models (e.g., `openai/gpt-5.4` on OpenRouter) sit in config returning 400s undetected
- Fallback chain health is invisible — broken fallback models only surface when the primary fails
- Users with 10-15+ models across tiers have no pre-flight check for the full config

## Changes
- `src/cli/models-cli.ts`: Register `--all` flag, pass `probeAll` to command handler
- `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts`: Pass `probeAll` through to `runAuthProbes` options
- `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts`: Add `probeAll` to `AuthProbeOptions`; when set, iterate all candidate models for each provider instead of picking one via `selectProbeModel()`
- `src/commands/models/list.probe.targets.test.ts`: 3 new vitest cases asserting probeAll behavior at the `buildProbeTargets` boundary

## Usage
```bash
# Probe every configured model
openclaw models status --probe --all

# Probe all models under a specific provider
openclaw models status --probe --all --probe-provider openrouter

# Existing behavior unchanged
openclaw models status --probe
```

## Backward compatibility
- `--probe` without `--all` behaves exactly as before (one model per provider)
- `--all` without `--probe` is a no-op (probing must be enabled)
- All existing CLI flags (`--probe-provider`, `--probe-timeout`, `--probe-concurrency`, `--probe-max-tokens`) work with `--all`

## Test plan
- [x] `openclaw models status --probe` — unchanged output: asserted in `list.probe.targets.test.ts::"defaults to the first configured candidate when probeAll is omitted"`. Given 3 configured anthropic models, `probeAll` absent → exactly 1 target emitted (the first candidate, asserted by model id, not just count).
- [x] `openclaw models status --probe --all` — every configured model surfaces: asserted in `list.probe.targets.test.ts::"expands to every configured candidate when probeAll is true"`. Given 3 configured anthropic models + `probeAll: true`, all 3 targets emitted (exact model id list asserted via sorted `toEqual`).
- [x] Fallback when no explicit candidates but catalog has one: asserted in `list.probe.targets.test.ts::"falls back to selectProbeModel when probeAll is true but no candidates are configured"` — catalog entry is used, exact model id asserted.
- [ ] `openclaw models status --probe --all --probe-provider anthropic` — filters to one provider: CLI-surface test, not covered by the probe-targets unit test. Manual repro: works because `buildProbeTargets` has always honored the `provider` filter; this PR only adds the `probeAll` expansion within an already-filtered provider.
- [ ] `openclaw models status --probe --all --json` — JSON output includes all model results: CLI-surface test, not covered by the probe-targets unit test. Manual repro: `runAuthProbes` returns the full target array which the JSON renderer already iterates; no code path added here.
- [ ] Deprecated model IDs show `error` / `no_model` status: behavioral test requires live provider auth and catalog. The `probeAll` code path exposes every candidate to the existing probe-and-classify logic; the `error`/`no_model` classification itself is unchanged in this PR.

```
$ pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/models/list.probe.targets.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)  (6 pre-existing + 3 new)
```

The last 3 items in the test plan remain manual CLI-integration checks (flag-level, JSON renderer, live-auth deprecated-model behavior). The core behavior this PR adds — `probeAll` expanding one-per-provider to every-candidate — is now CI-verified at the `buildProbeTargets` boundary with exact model-id assertions.

Implements #63145.

target.title: fix(memory): stabilize corpus supplement ordering for prompt-cache and performance stability
target.body:
## Summary

  - **Problem:** `listMemoryCorpusSupplements()` returned supplements in insertion order, which varies with plugin load sequence across restarts.
  - **Why it matters:** Non-deterministic ordering has two concrete effects:
    1. `getMemoryCorpusSupplementResult` iterates supplements sequentially and returns the first hit — if two supplements can serve the same path, registration order silently decides which one wins, making behavior inconsistent across restarts.
    2. `loader.ts` snapshots and restores the corpus supplement list for cache/rollback paths — an unstable order means cache hits can restore a different list than the live run produced.
    The parallel `promptSupplements` path already has an explicit `.toSorted()` with a comment explaining this exact concern; `corpusSupplements` was missing the same guard.
  - **What changed:** Added `.toSorted((a, b) => a.pluginId.localeCompare(b.pluginId))` to `listMemoryCorpusSupplements()` — matching the existing pattern in the same file.
  - **What did NOT change:** Registration mutation logic, supplement content, search result ordering (search uses score-based sort regardless), or any other part of `memory-state.ts`.

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [x] Bug fix

  ## Scope (select all touched areas)

  - [x] Memory / storage

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  ## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: `promptSupplements` was sorted when `corpusSupplements` was added later; the sort was not applied consistently.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: No test asserted that `listMemoryCorpusSupplements` returns a stable order regardless of registration sequence.
  - Contributing context (if known): The existing `promptSupplements` sort already has an explanatory comment; `corpusSupplements` was a copy without the guard.

  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [x] Unit test
  - Target test or file: `src/plugins/memory-state.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: reverse-alphabetical registration order produces alphabetical output; re-registering the same plugin does not duplicate or reorder entries.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The function is pure state; a unit test with controlled registration order is the fastest and most direct proof.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None (added in this PR).

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  None — `corpusSupplements` are consumed internally for memory search assembly; users do not see ordering directly. The fix eliminates silent prompt-cache invalidation.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```text
  Before:
  [plugins register in any order] -> listMemoryCorpusSupplements() -> insertion order (non-deterministic)

  After:
  [plugins register in any order] -> listMemoryCorpusSupplements() -> sorted by pluginId (stable)

  Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
  - New/changed network calls? No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? No
  - Data access scope changed? No

  Repro + Verification

  Steps

  1. Register two corpus supplements with IDs "plugin-z" and "plugin-a" (in that order).
  2. Call listMemoryCorpusSupplements().
  3. Before fix: order was ["plugin-z", "plugin-a"]. After fix: ["plugin-a", "plugin-z"].

  Expected

  Alphabetical order regardless of registration sequence.

  Actual (before fix)

  Insertion order.

  Evidence

  - Failing test/log before + passing after

  Two new tests in src/plugins/memory-state.test.ts — both pass after the fix, both would fail against the old implementation. Full suite: 13/13 green.

  Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: out-of-order registration, re-registration of existing plugin ID.
  - Edge cases checked: single supplement (trivially stable), re-registration replaces existing entry.
  - What you did not verify: live multi-plugin startup with real plugin packages.

  Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? Yes
  - Config/env changes? No
  - Migration needed? No

  Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: Any caller that depended on insertion order would observe a different sequence.
    - Mitigation: No public contract documents an insertion-order guarantee; the parallel promptSupplements path already sorts. No known callers depend on insertion order.

target.title: fix: honor filePath/path/media fallbacks in outbound reply normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- `normalizeOutboundReplyPayload` only checked `payload.mediaUrl`, silently dropping media when agents returned `filePath`, `path`, or `media` fields
- This caused WhatsApp (and other channel) auto-replies to send plain text instead of attachments (`hasMedia: false` in outbound logs)
- Added the same fallback chain already used by the message-send action runtime (`media` → `mediaUrl` → `filePath` → `path`)

## Root cause

The action runtime in `channel-DuaAdRTE.js` correctly maps all four parameter names:
```ts
const mediaUrl = readStringParam(params, "media") 
  ?? readStringParam(params, "mediaUrl")
  ?? readStringParam(params, "filePath")
  ?? readStringParam(params, "path");
```

But `normalizeOutboundReplyPayload` in `reply-payload.ts` only checked `mediaUrl`:
```ts
const mediaUrl = typeof payload.mediaUrl === "string" ? payload.mediaUrl : undefined;
```

This meant that when an agent's conversational reply included media via `filePath` or `path` (common when using tool results), the auto-reply delivery path silently dropped the attachment.

## Test plan

- [x] Added 6 unit tests covering all fallback paths and precedence
- [x] All 30 tests in `reply-payload.test.ts` pass
- [ ] Verify WhatsApp attachment sends work with `filePath` parameter on a live gateway

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(logging): route subagent announce output through subsystem logger
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway file logs record full subagent announce/completion assistant response bodies as `info` lines, polluting operational logs with chat content.
- Why it matters: Logs become noisy and hard to debug; large multi-line summaries bloat file size; user-facing text mixed into system logs is misleading and a potential privacy concern.
- What changed: Announce delivery output now routes through `createSubsystemLogger("agents/announce")` — metadata at `info`, full body at `debug`. Announce-related error/warning logging in `subagent-announce.ts` and `subagent-announce-delivery.ts` also moved from `defaultRuntime` to the subsystem logger.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Normal (non-announce) agent delivery logging, channel delivery, session transcript writes, or any LLM behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #62500
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `deliverAgentCommandResult()` in `delivery.ts` calls `runtime.log(output)` for announce run payloads. In the gateway process, `runtime.log()` → `console.log()` → `enableConsoleCapture()` intercepts all `console.log` calls and forwards them to the tslog root logger at `info` level via `getLogger().info(formatted)`, which writes to the rolling date file.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No distinction between announce-internal output and normal agent output in the delivery logging path. Announce runs use the same `logPayload()` / `logNestedOutput()` path as user-facing agent runs.
- Contributing context (if known): The announce delivery files (`subagent-announce.ts`, `subagent-announce-delivery.ts`) had no subsystem logger at all — errors went through `defaultRuntime.error()` which also hits the console capture path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: When `inputProvenance.sourceTool === "subagent_announce"`, `runtime.log()` must NOT receive the full payload body; instead `announceLog.info()` receives metadata and `announceLog.debug()` receives the full body.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Tests the exact decision point in `logPayload()` without requiring a live gateway or real subagent completion.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this gap is why the log pollution went undetected.
- If no new test is added, why not: A new test is added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw logs` output no longer includes full subagent completion prose at default log level.
- Announce metadata (session, runId, payload size) still appears at `info`.
- Full announce body is available at `debug` level for troubleshooting.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[subagent completes] → deliverAgentCommandResult → runtime.log(fullBody)
  → console.log(fullBody) → enableConsoleCapture → getLogger().info(fullBody)
  → openclaw-YYYY-MM-DD.log (info, no subsystem, full chat text)

After:
[subagent completes] → deliverAgentCommandResult → announceLog.info(metadata)
  → logToFile(fileLogger, "info", metadata)    ← clean operational log
  → announceLog.debug(fullBody)
  → logToFile(fileLogger, "debug", fullBody)   ← only at debug level
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: Any
- Integration/channel (if any): Any channel with subagent spawning

### Steps

1. Start a gateway with `logging.level: debug` or default `info`
2. Spawn a subagent that produces a multi-line completion summary
3. Let the subagent complete and announce back to the parent session
4. Inspect gateway logs via `openclaw logs --plain --local-time`

### Expected

- Gateway file logs show only announce metadata (session, runId, chars) at `info` level
- Full completion body appears only at `debug` level

### Actual

- Before this fix: full completion body appears at `info` level with no subsystem tag
- After this fix: metadata-only at `info`, full body at `debug`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - New unit test confirms announce payloads route through subsystem logger instead of `runtime.log`
  - All 14 existing announce-related tests pass (delivery + announce + announce-delivery)
  - `pnpm check` lint/format clean
- Edge cases checked:
  - Non-announce agent runs still use the original `runtime.log()` path (unchanged)
  - `opts.json` mode still uses `runtime.log(JSON.stringify(...))` (unchanged)
  - Nested non-announce runs still use `logNestedOutput()` (unchanged)
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full end-to-end gateway run with a real subagent completion (verified via code path analysis and unit test)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Announce errors previously written via `defaultRuntime.error()` now go through subsystem logger, which uses `rawConsole` instead of patched console.
  - Mitigation: Both paths write to stderr and file log at error level. The only difference is the subsystem logger adds a `[agents/announce]` tag, which is an improvement for filtering.

---

> **AI-assisted:** Built with Claude. All changes reviewed and understood. 14/14 related tests passing.

target.title: doctor(memory): surface FTS5 unavailable state in doctor checks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #62328 by making `openclaw doctor` visibly report when SQLite FTS5 is unavailable in the running gateway.

- Extend `doctor.memory.status` payload with `fts` probe fields (`enabled`, `available`, `error`)
- Thread that payload through `probeGatewayMemoryStatus` in doctor command flow
- Add a dedicated doctor warning in `noteMemorySearchHealth` when gateway reports FTS unavailable
- Include targeted guidance for `no such module: fts5` / missing `ENABLE_FTS5`

## Why

Without this, users can see memory embeddings as ready while FTS keyword/BM25 fallback is silently unavailable. This change surfaces the condition and gives concrete next steps.

## Tests

- Added/updated test in `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts` to assert `fts` is returned in `doctor.memory.status`
- Added test in `src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts` to assert doctor emits an FTS5 warning when gateway probe reports unavailable
- Local targeted verification:
  - `corepack pnpm exec vitest src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts -t "returns gateway embedding probe status for the default agent"`
  - `corepack pnpm exec vitest src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts -t "warns when gateway probe reports FTS unavailable"`

## Notes

One unrelated existing test in `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts` is currently path-sensitive on this Windows environment (`falls back to the manager workspace...`) and fails independently of this change. The new/affected targeted cases pass.

target.title: feat(cron): add preHook gate for pre-run script checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an optional `preHook` configuration for cron jobs and heartbeat triggers that runs a shell command before agent execution. This gates agent runs on system preconditions (disk space, network, API availability) — eliminating wasteful runs on resource-constrained hosts.

- **Exit 0**: proceed with agent execution
- **Exit 10**: skip (not counted as a failure, `consecutiveErrors` stays at 0)
- **Any other**: error (increments `consecutiveErrors`, triggers backoff/alerts)

### Config example (cron)
```json5
{
  schedule: "cron 0 8 * * 1-5",
  preHook: {
    command: "ping -c1 -W2 api.openai.com > /dev/null 2>&1",
    timeoutSeconds: 10,
  },
}
```

### Config example (heartbeat)
```json5
{
  agents: {
    defaults: {
      heartbeat: {
        every: "30m",
        preHook: {
          command: "/usr/local/bin/check-should-run.sh",
          timeoutSeconds: 30,
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Changes

- **New**: `src/cron/pre-hook.ts` — core preHook runner with cross-platform shell support, timeout, maxBuffer handling, AbortSignal forwarding
- **New**: `src/cron/pre-hook.test.ts` — 9 tests covering all exit paths
- **Types**: `preHook` field on `CronJobBase` (flows to CronJob/Create/Patch), heartbeat config, zod schema
- **Cron integration**: preHook gate in `executeJobCore()` before session-target dispatch; AbortSignal propagated from timer through server-cron bridge to heartbeat runner
- **Heartbeat integration**: preHook gate after preflight + session lane check, before agent turn; emits heartbeat events on skip/error
- **Normalization**: validates command string, clamps timeoutSeconds 1-300, rejects bare strings/primitives
- **Protocol schemas**: `CronPreHookSchema` added to CronJob, CronAdd, CronJobPatch (nullable in patch)
- **Tool schemas**: `preHook` in `CRON_RECOVERABLE_OBJECT_KEYS` and both add/update tool schemas
- **Docs**: pre-run hooks sections in cron-jobs.md, heartbeat.md, configuration-reference.md

## Review feedback addressed (from prior PRs #17529, #49415, #53789, #54239)

| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|-------|-----|
| 1 | maxBuffer unknown exit treated as skip | Now always "error" |
| 2 | preHook ran before preflight | Placed after preflight + session lane check |
| 3 | stdout/stderr missing from logs | Included in both cron and heartbeat log context |
| 4 | AbortSignal not propagated | Threaded through state.ts → server-cron.ts → heartbeat-runner.ts |
| 5 | No abort check before spawn | Checked in pre-hook.ts before execFile |
| 6 | Missing heartbeat event on skip/error | emitHeartbeatEvent on both paths |
| 7 | Docs exit-code mismatch | Corrected in all 3 doc files |
| 8 | Bare string preHook accepted | Rejected in normalize.ts |
| 9 | Typed tool schemas missing preHook | Added to both add and update schemas |

Closes #62185

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/pre-hook.test.ts` — 9/9 passing
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/normalize.test.ts` — 60/60 passing (10 new preHook tests)
- [x] `pnpm check` — no new errors (12 pre-existing on main)
- [ ] `pnpm test` — full suite
- [ ] Manual: cron job with `preHook: { command: "exit 0" }` → job runs
- [ ] Manual: `preHook: { command: "exit 10" }` → job skipped, no error count
- [ ] Manual: `preHook: { command: "exit 1" }` → job blocked, errors increment

target.title: CLI: prefer bundled installs for scoped plugin specs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/feishu` can create a duplicate global plugin copy even when the same plugin is already bundled.
- Why it matters: the install command reports success, but runtime discovery/load still prefers the bundled plugin, so the newly installed global copy does not actually take effect.
- What changed: scoped npm specs now participate in the bundled pre-install resolution path, so installs like `@openclaw/feishu` resolve to the bundled plugin by default instead of creating a duplicate global install.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not change runtime duplicate resolution or plugin origin precedence during discovery/load.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw plugins install @openclaw/<plugin>` now prefers a matching bundled plugin before attempting npm installation.

If a bundled plugin matches the requested npm spec, OpenClaw enables the bundled plugin instead of creating a duplicate global plugin install.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): bundled plugin install flow
- Relevant config (redacted): default local config

### Steps

1. Use a build that bundles `@openclaw/feishu`.
2. Run `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/feishu`.
3. Observe whether OpenClaw creates a duplicate global plugin copy or resolves to bundled.

### Expected

- A matching bundled plugin should be selected by default.
- No duplicate global plugin copy should be created for the same plugin id.

### Actual

- Before this change, scoped npm specs bypassed the bundled pre-install path.
- This could create a duplicate global install that runtime later marked as overridden by the bundled plugin.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test src/cli/plugin-install-plan.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/plugins/bundled-sources.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/commands/onboarding/plugin-install.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
  - `pnpm build`
- Edge cases checked:
  - bare plugin-id specs still resolve to bundled
  - scoped npm specs now resolve to bundled when matched
  - unmatched scoped npm specs still return no bundled pre-plan
  - existing onboarding and loader behavior still passes unchanged
- What I did **not** verify:
  - full `pnpm check` is currently blocked by unrelated existing `extensions/tlon/**` type-resolution errors for `@tloncorp/api`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR
- Files/config to restore: `src/cli/plugin-install-plan.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - scoped bundled plugin installs unexpectedly falling through to npm install again

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - A scoped npm package could now resolve to bundled earlier than before.
- Mitigation:
  - The change is limited to explicit bundled matches and is covered by targeted tests.

## AI Assistance

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the code changes and ran the verification commands listed above.

target.title: status: avoid misleading default model when agent defaults vary
target.body:
## Summary
`openclaw status` summarized session defaults from `agents.defaults.model` only, which made explicit `agents.list[*].model` overrides look ignored in status output.

## Root cause
`getStatusSummary()` resolved `sessions.defaults.model` without agent context, so multi-agent configs collapsed to the global default model in `status`, `status --json`, and the TUI summary.

## Fix
- derive the status session-default summary from each agent’s effective default model
- keep the concrete model display when all agents resolve to the same effective default
- report `varies by agent` when agent-effective defaults differ
- carry that state through the CLI and TUI renderers

## Tests
- regression for divergent per-agent default models
- regression for the non-divergent case
